title: What is the status of support for 3D arrays in Verilator nowadays?
body:
I have seen comments in the code saying that 3D arrays are not dealt with, though I cannot find those again just now .. ah, here is something along those lines:

          
> We might be assigning a 1-D packed array to a 2-D packed array,
>     this is unsupported.

in V3Slice.cpp. Oh yes, got it:

```
           if (pdim > 1 || udim > 1) {
                puts("//UNSUP ");  // VerilatedImp can't deal with >2d or packed arrays
            }
```

in V3EmitCSyms.cpp .


Do those comments still apply (the last obviously does!) and what is their impact?

PS. I have noticed  readmem reads incorrectly when the memory cell size is 128. It works fine when the cell size is 32. I have not investigated ... other than to read the code and decide it is impenetratable. I noticed it was divided up into different object types per "single", "double", "quad" and other, and could not get any further ... no clue even where those are defined or what they were derived from, and I am paraphrasing the actual name of the class/template whatever, which was something like QOYPOY as far as I recall! Please write with meaningful names that indicate a coherent concept as a class/object, so the code is maintainable and accessible - and those two are the same thing! The more accessible it is, the more help you get, so it is in your interest. Maintenance, perfective, corrective or otherwise, is 95% of the effort in coding.
